posts.title: The four-hundred-year-old document.
posts.summary: The bill of lading, and why paper still governs a $33 trillion market. MLETR and the UK Electronic Trade Documents Act 2023 give electronic trade documents legal standing for the first time — a once-in-four-centuries transition, chartable jurisdiction by jurisdiction.
inside: Why the bill of lading survived four centuries of technology
MLETR and the ETDA 2023 as the legal unlock, dated and chartable
What MLETR-compliant on a public chain concretely means, via TradeTrust

posts.title: The value accrual problem.
posts.summary: Does document tokenization create token demand? The three demand channels — gas, masternode collateral, protocol collateral — against the USDC settlement problem and TradeTrust's chain-agnosticism. The framework's hardest question, placed early on purpose.
inside: The three demand channels for the native token, examined one by one
Settlement through Circle's rails: the inverted-RLUSD structure
Supply overhang and the formal statement of the elasticity question

posts.title: The consortium graveyard.
posts.summary: we.trade, Marco Polo, Contour: blockchain trade finance backed by the world's largest banks failed three times in two years. An autopsy of each — and an honest accounting of which failure causes MLETR, public-chain neutrality, and TFDi actually cure, and which they do not.
inside: Three autopsies: we.trade, Marco Polo Network, Contour
The failure causes: governance, incentives, liquidity islands, no legal substrate
The differentiation argument, taken only as far as it honestly goes

posts.title: Sizing the machine.
posts.summary: A TVL-linked accrual model: for a given volume of on-chain trade finance, derive native-token demand from staking collateral, fee dynamics, and protocol collateral. Scenario tables from market penetration to implied price — with the elasticity assumption exposed, not buried.
inside: The TVL-linked accrual model, built channel by channel
Scenario table: penetration × chain share × elasticity → implied market cap
Where the square-root law does apply: absorbing institutional positions

posts.title: The case against, taken seriously.
posts.summary: Five failure modes at full strength, from accrual decoupling to a consortium-graveyard repeat. Probability table, expected value, Kelly sizing, and explicit falsification criteria. The bull case is earned only by taking the bear case on its own terms.
inside: Five failure modes, each at full strength
Probability table, expected value, and Kelly sizing
The falsification criteria that would retire this framework
